old_claim.note: Green Hydrogen provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: A manufactured electrolyser was the required apparatus that converted renewable electricity and water into hydrogen in the Utsira system.

source_shape.source_locator: The Utsira system description identifies an electrolyser as the component converting wind-generated electricity into hydrogen, alongside hydrogen storage and reconversion equipment.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Green-hydrogen production used electrolyser hardware; manufacturing that hardware does not depend on the green-hydrogen application category.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The previous edge reversed product and application: electrolysers existed for industrial hydrogen before green hydrogen, while an electrolysis-based green-hydrogen system requires an electrolyser.

ontology_before: Electrolyser manufacturing incorrectly depended on green hydrogen, while green hydrogen omitted the apparatus that performs electrolysis.
ontology_after: Green hydrogen requires electrolyser manufacturing, and the reverse edge is removed.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-grid-battery-green-hydrogen-boundary: green_hydrogen requires electrolyzer_manufacturing and the reverse edge is absent.
would_reject_if: The green_hydrogen node is broadened to include non-electrolytic renewable hydrogen pathways.
Evidence establishes that manufacturing electrolysers historically required the green-hydrogen market category.
short_reason: Electrolyser hardware enables green-hydrogen production; the application does not enable manufacture of the apparatus.
